title: brickellia veronicifolia var. umbratilis
intro: Brickellia veronicifolia var. umbratilis, also known as Mexican brickellia or variegated brickellia, is a plant species that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Brickellia veronicifolia var. umbratilis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and alleviating respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture for its medicinal purposes, and its leaves can also be used fresh in salads or as a garnish for their unique flavor and aroma.
